questions.question: A box contains a total of $90$ discs which are numbered from $1$ to $90$. If one disc is drawn at random from the box, find the probability that it bears: (i) a two-digit number, (ii) a perfect square number, and (iii) a number divisible by $5$.

steps: Identify the total number of outcomes $n(S) = 90$.
For (i), identify the two-digit numbers from $1$ to $90$. These are numbers from $10$ to $90$. The count is $90 - 10 + 1 = 81$. Calculate $P(E_1) = \frac{81}{90}$.

For (ii), identify perfect squares between $1$ and $90$. These are $1^2, 2^2, 3^2, 4^2, 5^2, 6^2, 7^2, 8^2, 9^2$ ($1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, 81$). The count is $9$. Calculate $P(E_2) = \frac{9}{90}$.

For (iii), identify multiples of $5$ between $1$ and $90$. This is an arithmetic progression $5, 10, \dots, 90$. Using $a_n = a + (n-1)d$, $90 = 5 + (n-1)5$, so $18 = n$. The count is $18$. Calculate $P(E_3) = \frac{18}{90}$.
Simplify all fractions to their lowest terms.
solution.derivation: n(S) = 90 \\ (i) \text{ Two-digit numbers: } \{10, 11, \dots, 90\}, n(E_1) = 81 \Rightarrow P(E_1) = \frac{81}{90} = \frac{9}{10} \\ (ii) \text{ Perfect squares: } \{1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, 81\}, n(E_2) = 9 \Rightarrow P(E_2) = \frac{9}{90} = \frac{1}{10} \\ (iii) \text{ Divisible by } 5: \{5, 10, \dots, 90\}, n(E_3) = \frac{90}{5} = 18 \Rightarrow P(E_3) = \frac{18}{90} = \frac{1}{5}
